title: Change rollback execution evidence — declared rollback vs actual
exclusions: Does NOT evaluate whether the change itself was authorised (covered by 22.42.1); focuses on rollback readiness.
evidence: Saved search 'change_rollback_evidence' (weekly) emits CRs whose declared rollback plan was never exercised during the test window.

compliance.assurance_rationale: CM-3 configuration-change control requires tested rollback.
CC8.1 change-management evidence requires tested rollback.
SOX ITGC rollback evidence is satisfied in a risk-based manner.

controlTest.positiveScenario: A CR claims a rollback plan exists but the rollback pipeline was never invoked in its 6-month test cadence; the UC fires.
controlTest.negativeScenario: A CR whose rollback was exercised (pipeline_event=rollback_test success) does NOT fire.

dataSources: ITSM CR records (ServiceNow), CI/CD pipeline audit (rollback_test events).
spl:
| inputlookup itsm_changes.csv
| where rollback_plan_required="true"
| lookup rollback_tests.csv change_id OUTPUT last_tested
| eval age_days=round((now()-strptime(last_tested,"%Y-%m-%d"))/86400,0)
| where isnull(last_tested) OR age_days>180
| table change_id rollback_plan_required last_tested age_days
description: Continuously verifies that every change with a declared rollback plan has exercised that plan within policy cadence (default 180 days).
value: Turns rollback-plan existence from a documentation artefact into a tested capability.
grandmaExplanation: Turns rollback-plan existence from a documentation artefact into a tested capability — so you can show exactly what is being watched and when something went wrong.
implementation: (1) Export CR metadata to itsm_changes.csv; (2) have CI/CD emit rollback_test events; (3) run weekly; (4) on miss, schedule a rollback rehearsal.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: ITSM CR records (ServiceNow), CI/CD pipeline audit (rollback_test events)..
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Export CR metadata to itsm_changes.csv; (2) have CI/CD emit rollback_test events; (3) run weekly; (4) on miss, schedule a rollback rehearsal.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| inputlookup itsm_changes.csv
| where rollback_plan_required="true"
| lookup rollback_tests.csv change_id OUTPUT last_tested
| eval age_days=round((now()-strptime(last_tested,"%Y-%m-%d"))/86400,0)
| where isnull(last_tested) OR age_days>180
| table change_id rollback_plan_required last_tested age_days
```

Understanding this SPL

**Change rollback execution evidence — declared rollback vs actual** — Turns rollback-plan existence from a documentation artefact into a tested capability.

Documented **Data sources**: ITSM CR records (ServiceNow), CI/CD pipeline audit (rollback_test events). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Loads rows via `inputlookup` (KV store or CSV lookup) for enrichment or reporting.
• Filters the current rows with `where rollback_plan_required="true"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• `eval` defines or adjusts **age_days** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where isnull(last_tested) OR age_days>180` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **Change rollback execution evidence — declared rollback vs actual**): table change_id rollback_plan_required last_tested age_days


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Aging bar chart of changes without rollback test, table of at-risk CRs, single-value 'CRs with tested rollback'.
visualization: Aging bar chart of changes without rollback test, table of at-risk CRs, single-value 'CRs with tested rollback'.
knownFalsePositives: Emergency hotfix CRs may legitimately have a 'forward-fix-only' rollback strategy; annotate with rollback_type to exclude them.
